meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS build. Master this professional client workflow to ship faster, monetize, and overcome project fatigue using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To stop stalling, implement a rigid client-facing feedback loop. Treat your MVP as a service contract: define narrow scopes, automate technical debt management with Claude Code, and use Antigravity to sync requirements with production. By separating feature delivery from infrastructure, you maintain momentum and ensure every sprint results in billable value.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks—perhaps months—in the 'almost done' graveyard. The codebase is a tangled mess of half-baked features, and the excitement of the initial idea has been replaced by the crushing weight of technical debt and feature creep. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a production-hardened client workflow.

Professional SaaS founders don't 'code'; they orchestrate systems. By shifting your mindset from individual contributor to lead architect, you can cut the noise and focus on high-leverage deliverables. This checklist provides the exact framework to move your product from a local repo to a revenue-generating asset, utilizing modern AI-assisted engineering.

steps.title: Define the Revenue-First Scope
steps.description: Strip your project down to the one feature that solves a burning pain for a specific client. Stop building features for 'maybe' users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the single feature that provides the most immediate value to a paying customer. Suggest a scope reduction to MVP status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify and document the core MVP feature set based on current codebase logic.'

steps.title: Establish the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Create a formal client review cycle before writing a single line of code. Validate your UI/UX assumptions with actual stakeholders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a structured feedback checklist for my beta clients based on the feature requirements documented in the repo.

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold recurring client components, ensuring consistency and reducing the time spent on UI configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular dashboard component architecture that allows for easy client-specific customization without modifying core business logic.

steps.title: Implement Continuous Deployment Sync
steps.description: Use Antigravity to bridge the gap between your local development and the client's staging environment, ensuring zero-friction updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an automated deployment pipeline that triggers on feature completion and updates the staging environment documentation.

steps.title: Finalize Monetization Hooks
steps.description: Integrate billing and usage tracking as a first-class citizen in your code. If it isn't tracked, it isn't a business.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the codebase for missing telemetry or billing hooks. Inject Stripe-ready usage tracking into all core feature endpoints.

pro_tips: Never ship a feature without a corresponding 'kill-switch' flag that allows you to disable it if it breaks production.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests *before* the feature code; it prevents the 'I'll add tests later' trap.
If a feature takes more than 3 days to build, it's too complex for your current MVP; break it into smaller sub-modules.
Treat your client feedback as a 'requirement change' and force a commit history that reflects these shifts for auditability.
Automate your changelog generation via Claude Code; it forces you to acknowledge progress every single day.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is deployed, immediately schedule a demo with a potential buyer. Do not write a second feature until the first one has been validated by a paid user.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a private repository. Implement this workflow, tighten your feedback loops, and start shipping software that matters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
